summary: This cluster of research focused on the phylogenetic reassessment of the desmid genus *Staurastrum* and its close relatives within the Chlorophyta, using mitochondrial coxIII gene sequence analysis as the primary methodological tool. The studies reevaluated the taxonomic boundaries of the genus *Staurodesmus* and compared its evolutionary distance to *Xanthidium* and *Arthrodesmus*, concluding that *Staurastrum* is more closely related to *Xanthidium* than to *Staurodesmus*. The geographic and ecological scope was not explicitly defined in the summaries, but the work centered on resolving the evolutionary relationships among these morphologically similar freshwater green algae.
